title: adhatoda carthaginensis
intro: Adhatoda carthaginensis, also known as Adhatoda vasica, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and parts of Africa. It is a medicinal plant known for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and sore throats, and its health benefits include reduced inflammation and improved respiratory function. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Adhatoda carthaginensis are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
